title: Under local vote semantics, inspect /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public for stale docs or prompt/check text that still claims assist defaults to claude or that codex+claude+glm is the default baseline after our Claude-free code change. Focus on README.md, docs/gha24-mainframe-flow.md, docs...
summary_text: ### Recommended highest-value fix Target file: `scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh` 1. **Stop fabricating `KERNEL_RUN_ID` as `unknown-run` in doctor paths** - Change `cmd_doctor` logic that sets `doctor_focus_run_id="${KERNEL_RUN_ID:-unknown-run}"` to: - if a focused/explicit run id is missing or invalid, leave it empty. - emit `no-active-run-context` (or similar) instead of forcing `unknown-run`. - This prevents fake per-run diagnostics and removes the main source of noisy output. 2. **Gate run-scoped diagnostics with explicit validity checks** - Add a small check before each run-targeted status call (compact/bootstrap/receipts/runtime/health): - validate run-id format (`non-empty`, sane chars), - verify existence of at least one run artifact or compact/receipt file. - If invalid/missing, print a single explicit warning like `run-context-unresolved` and skip that scoped check. 3. **Keep stale/real issues visible** - Retain all existing per-run checks when a concrete, valid run id is found. - For explicit `--run` invocation: - fail fast with clear `invalid-or-stale-run-id` if target run cannot be resolved. - avoid invoking downstream scripts with `unknown-run` placeholders. ### Behavior change summary (exact) - Before: - `doctor` with no resolveable run often emits diagnostics tagged `unknown-run` from multiple subsystems. - After: - `doctor` with no valid run context will show a concise “no active/valid run id” message and only global checks. - `doctor --run <bogus>` will immediately return explicit invalid/run-missing error. - Known valid runs continue to produce existing full diagnostics unchanged. ### Focused test suggestions 1. **No runs present** - Run: `./scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh doctor` - Expect: no `unknown-run` in output; clear no-run-context message; no fake bootstrap/runtime sub-sections for unknown run. 2. **Invalid explicit run id** - Run: `./scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh doctor --run unknown-run` (or random non-existent id) - Expect: one clear “run id unresolved/invalid” diagnostic, non-zero exit, no downstream `unknown-run` noise. 3. **Explicit valid run** - Run with a real run id. - Expect: unchanged per-run diagnostics and behavior for bootstrap/compact/runtime/health for that id. 4. **Active run discovery path** - Run: `./scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh doctor` when compact/receipt exists. - Expect: it resolves to real run id and emits per-run checks for that id only. 5. **`--all-runs` mixed stale/active** - Run: `./scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh doctor --all-runs` - Expect: real runs reported as before; stale/missing run contexts should be explicit and not fabricated as `unknown-run`.
